title: Uv led waveguide system with scattering for anti-fouling
abstract: The invention provides a waveguide element (1000) comprising a first face (1001) and a second face (1002) with radiation transmissive material (1005) configured between the first face (1001) and the second face (1002), wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) is transmissive for UV radiation, wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) is a matrix material for a phase (1010) of another composition than the radiation transmissive material (1005), wherein the phase (1010) is available in the matrix as regions (1110) with the regions (1110) having mean particle radii (r1) selected from the range of 50-1500 nm and having an average region concentration selected from the range of 1*10 4 â1.5*108/ mm 3 of the radiation transmissive material (1005).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A waveguide element (1000) comprising radiation transmissive material (1005), wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) is transmissive for UV radiation, wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) is a matrix material for a phase (1010) of another composition than the radiation transmissive material (1005), wherein the phase (1010) is available in the matrix as regions (1110) with the regions (1110) having mean particle radii (rl) selected from the range of 50-1500 nm and having an average region concentration selected from the range of 1 * 10 4 - 1.5* 10 8 / mm 3 of the radiation transmissive material (1005), and having a volume fraction of the regions (1110) selected from the range of 0.00003- 3 vol.%. 2. The waveguide element (1000) according to claim 1, wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) comprises one or more of a silicone and a fluoropolymer. 3. The waveguide element (1000) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or more regions (1110) consist of at least 80% vol.% of a gas. 4. The waveguide element (1000) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or more regions (1110) consist of at least 80% vol.% of water. 5. The waveguide element (1000) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein one or more regions (1110) comprise solid particles selected from the group consisting of silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, boron nitride, magnesium oxide, barium sulphate, calcium carbonate, zirconium dioxide and zinc oxide. 6. The waveguide element (1000) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) and the phase (1010) have an absolute difference of indices of refraction selected from the range of 0.04-0.8, wherein the waveguide element (1000) has a thickness (hi) defined by the distance between a first face (1001) and a second face (1002) selected from the range of 0.5-200 mm, wherein the radiation 

 transmissive material (1005) is configured between the first face (1001) and the second face (1002), and wherein the radiation transmissive material (1005) comprises a silicone. 7. The waveguide element (1000) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the regions (1110) have mean particle radii (rl) in nm and an average region concentration (cl) in /mm 3 defined by the range cl = a*(10 b )*(x d ), wherein 0.5<a<4; 7<b<15; -3.0<d<-1.0; x=rl; and 100 nm <rl< 1000 nm. 8. An anti-bio fouling system (200) comprising a light source (10) configured to provide UV radiation (11) and the waveguide element (1000) according to any one of the preceding claims 1-7, wherein the light source (10) and the waveguide element (1000) are adapted for propagation of at least part of the UV radiation (11) through the radiation transmissive material (1005) and subsequent emanation from at least one of a first face (1001) and a second face (1002). 9. The anti-bio fouling system (200) according to claim 8, wherein the light source (10) is configured external from the waveguide element (1000). 10. The anti-bio fouling system (200) according to any one of the preceding claims 8-9, wherein the light source (10) is at least partly embedded in the waveguide element (1000), wherein the light source (10) has an optical axis (O), wherein the light source (10) is configured with the optical axis (O) perpendicular to one or more of the first face (1001) and the second face (1002). 11. The anti-bio fouling system (200) according to any one of the preceding claims 8-10, wherein the light source (10) is configured to provide UVC radiation. 12. The anti-bio fouling system (200) according to any one of the preceding claims 8-11, comprising a plurality of light sources (10), wherein each light source (10) has a light emitting surface (12), and wherein the light sources (10) have shortest distances (dl) between neighboring light emitting surfaces (12) selected from the range of 1-50 mm. 13. An object (2000) comprising an object surface (2110) and the anti-bio fouling system (200) according to any one of the preceding claims 8-12, wherein at least one of the 

 first face (1001) and the second face (1002) of said anti-bio fouling system (200) is configured as at least part of said object surface (2110), and wherein in a first state the anti- biofouling system (200) is configured to provide UV radiation (1 1) emanating from said at least one of the first face (1001) and a second face (1002). 14. The object (2000) according to claim 13, wherein the object (1000) is selected from the group consisting of an immobile marine object, a mobile marine object, an infrastructural element, and a windmill. 15. A method of providing an anti-bio fouling system (200) as defined in any one of claims 8-12 to an object (2000), that during use is at least temporarily exposed to water, the method comprising providing the anti-bio fouling system (200) to the object (2000) with the anti-bio fouling system (200) configured to provide said UV radiation (11) to one or more of a part (2111) of an object surface (2110) of the object (10) and water adjacent to said part (2111).
